task: 'this scroll can be better' -- a screenshot showed the Object Strip's plain native OS scrollbar, a boxy arrow-capped bar with nothing in common with the app's own warm rounded design language. Replaced with round arrow buttons flanking the strip plus a soft edge fade -- the underlying scroll is still genuine native overflow-x:auto (touch/trackpad/click-drag all keep working), only its own default chrome is hidden now (scrollbar-width:none / ::-webkit-scrollbar{display:none}), with scroll-behavior:smooth and scroll-snap-type:x proximity/scroll-snap-align:start added for a satisfying settle. js/objectStrip.js's init() wires the two new arrow buttons to listRoot.scrollBy({left,behavior:'smooth'}) by one page-width click, and a shared update() (called on scroll and after every refresh(), since content changes affect scrollWidth) disables each arrow at its real end, mirroring Screen 2's own carousel arrow-disabled-state convention. Verified via a new Playwright test (objstrip_scroll_test.js) forcing real overflow: native scrollbar confirmed hidden, Prev starts correctly disabled at the true start (an 8px tolerance absorbs scroll-snap's own sub-pixel settle), clicking Next genuinely scrolls and enables Prev, scrolling to the real end disables Next. Full regression across the existing Creator/Builder suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change.
